[2018-03-10 03:57] iminabearsuit: Lifecoach has been queuing into Tier X players non stop on his stream, surely that shouldn't be happening when you're tier 2? Wasn't there a hard cap on MMR differences, on who can queue into eachother?
[2018-03-10 04:00] mrguy888: He could wait forever instead.
[2018-03-10 04:00] mrguy888: He has been getting new players too.
[2018-03-10 04:00] jamesa7171: yeah, this is pretty :NotLikeThis: to watch, but hopefully as we get more new players that both unlock and decide to try ranked this won't be an issue going forward
[2018-03-10 04:01] iminabearsuit: It's just kind of demotivating to see him queue into opponents that are literal 0% win chance.
[2018-03-10 04:01] f300xen (f0x): as a new player, i'm sick of queueing into tier X players every other game
[2018-03-10 04:01] iminabearsuit: Like, he's bringing it up every other game, that he feels the matchmaking feels unfair.
[2018-03-10 04:04] wilson0862 (Wilson): If you queue now, you will queue into lifecoach maybe. :Q
[2018-03-10 04:06] gex__ (gex): Has anyone check one of his replays to see if the system thinks his MMR is close to master, or like >= 1000?
[2018-03-10 04:07] iminabearsuit: Was gonna grab the replaycode after his game against kops now.
[2018-03-10 04:09] wilson0862 (Wilson): People keep talking about Gwent and HS in his chat.
[2018-03-10 04:13] Deleted User (Deleted User): his rating is master tier surely right
[2018-03-10 04:14] Deleted User (Deleted User): i assume they prefer gwent/hs :NotLikeThis:
[2018-03-10 04:17] mrguy888: 1150 44 games played
[2018-03-10 04:17] mtanzer: I just checked that but too slow apparently
[2018-03-10 04:19] iminabearsuit: And he got queued against Celestia at one point, and she is 1800. 750 difference in points is, somewhat big.
[2018-03-10 04:20] mtanzer: the issue is it's either get large rating differences or wait a really long time for another low rated player to queue ranked
[2018-03-10 04:21] iminabearsuit: Which is why I assumed there would be a hardcap. I don't really think on a ranked ladder, a player should be matched against someone where they have an expected winrate below 10%.
[2018-03-10 04:23] wilson0862 (Wilson): But in an accurate system, they lose negligible points accordingly.
[2018-03-10 04:24] iminabearsuit: I think the morale points lost are more valuable than the potential ranked points gained.
[2018-03-10 04:24] mqp: i'm shocked that a clearly talented game player is only 1150 after 44 games
[2018-03-10 04:25] iminabearsuit: He learnt the game from the wiki.
[2018-03-10 04:25] mqp: he looked a little stronger than that, i would have guessed 1300 or 1400
[2018-03-10 04:25] mqp: maybe rating deflation has happened since i learned to play
[2018-03-10 04:25] iminabearsuit: He's having 2 huge issues. He doesn't block with drones, and keeps getting breached for no value. And he buys very inefficient units, because he's afraid to waste resources.
[2018-03-10 04:26] sunfallte (Sunfall): Yeah, I was gonna say that he regularly ignores blue and gets breached a ton when he shouldn't.  I'm not too surprised the game has him at 1150.
[2018-03-10 04:26] mrguy888: getting breached for no value isnt totally a not holding drones problem
[2018-03-10 04:27] mrguy888: he know that absorb is the best thing ever but after the first turn or two of opponent attack he just stops
[2018-03-10 04:27] iminabearsuit: Well, he's getting breached, because he's not holding drones.
[2018-03-10 04:27] iminabearsuit: He generally knows how to defend when he has resources for it.
[2018-03-10 04:29] mrguy888: Based on how good he has gotten at other games i am pretty surprised by the difficulties he is having.
[2018-03-10 04:30] mqp: i don't recall, but i'm pretty sure a lot of my learning process was asking questions and getting advice from other stronger players, so maybe the lack of doing that makes it much harder
[2018-03-10 04:30] iminabearsuit: Yeah, but I do believe it's those two basic issues that's been holding him back. And then he doesn't really understand why he lose, so he can't improve.
[2018-03-10 04:31] iminabearsuit: Like, his early game is generally solid. But in the mid game he just falls behind, and by the late game he gets overrun.
[2018-03-10 04:38] wilson0862 (Wilson): Does anybody really think he won't pick it up by the end of the weekend though? He is a beast.
[2018-03-10 04:38] wilson0862 (Wilson): Even just in terms of concentration.
[2018-03-10 04:47] sunfallte (Sunfall): Depends?  People can be weirdly hard-headed and I don't know enough about him.
[2018-03-10 04:47] sunfallte (Sunfall): But it'll probably be fine.
[2018-03-10 04:48] sunfallte (Sunfall): Some huge Spanish streamer now.
[2018-03-10 04:48] sunfallte (Sunfall): (huge as in viewcount)
[2018-03-10 04:50] elyot: The matchmaking shouldn't match that wide, the limit is 500, but low-ranked people get their ratings squashed upwards a bit otherwise they wait in queue forever
[2018-03-10 04:50] elyot: now that we have more people, I can reduce that squashing
[2018-03-10 04:50] elyot: I'm gonna make some small adjustments to the formula
[2018-03-10 04:50] mrguy888: display rating and matchmaking rating are a lot less related under tier x
[2018-03-10 04:51] mrguy888: tier x and higher display is 350 above mmr
[2018-03-10 04:51] mrguy888: below it is wonkier so over 500 isnt over 500

[2018-03-14 07:13] jamesa7171: we have discussed the SSP situation in the mods chat before. it is possible we are being too laissez-faire in general (and I would welcome an elaboration/discussion on "authoritarian community management" @http404error if you are open to that and have the time) but a sample size of 1 is not enough to base that on. (of course, there may be additional people besides SSP who you think we should have taken action against and that simply hasn't come up in discussion yet.)

personally, I have a hard time taking action against someone simply because the community thinks their opinions about the game are bad. if you imagine an alternate universe where every SSP unit was amazing and every SSP strategy tidbit was helpful, people would love him and this would not be an issue at all; it is only an issue because his units and strategy advice are widely condemned, but that to me should not be a deciding factor in the way we approach somebody's actions. I definitely do feel like moderating SSP would improve the chat experience for most people, but taking action against somebody who has not broken any rules purely based on a personal opinion that it would improve chat is IMO egregious mod overreach (likeability is a requirement to not get banned now?) and an extremely dangerous road to go down.

on the other hand, I definitely get the frustration surrounding #unit_and_game_design as I feel it too. I had hoped that http's unit design challenges would help - and not to say they haven't - but it's clear that entire channel is a bit of a mess at the moment. I have been thinking about possible ways to alter the channel itself to kind of "naturally" take care of the problem, and perhaps we need to just rate-limit posting new units or something. if anybody has any ideas please let us know
[2018-03-14 07:14] http404error: Rate-limiting sounds like a reasonable action to me
[2018-03-14 07:15] http404error: Discord simply isn't a great format for design challenges - that's just natural. Things scroll, discussions get crowded.
[2018-03-14 07:15] http404error: I think the spamminess of SSP's behavior is the most annoying and also the most actionable part
[2018-03-14 07:17] http404error: I don't know what the ideal solution is, if there exists one. The problem is that similar to previous situations, the result of no-action will be that most reasonable people (including myself) will leave and then all that's left will be cancer.
[2018-03-14 07:18] http404error: I kind of just want the channel to be reddit so people can downvote and move on, instead of it dominating the discussion.
[2018-03-14 07:21] http404error: My opinion is that someone doesn't have to be committing moral wrongs to be a negatively contributing community member. If I were king, I would simply weigh problems-caused versus value-created.
[2018-03-14 07:23] http404error: I wonder if it's even possible for productive game design discussions to exist on Discord
[2018-03-14 07:23] http404error: They don't really work without some kind of hierarchy of respect
[2018-03-14 07:23] http404error: Believe me, I've tried
[2018-03-14 07:24] Deleted User (Deleted User): hierarchy of respect?
[2018-03-14 07:25] http404error: There's no productive exchange of information unless one party has a reason to believe another.
[2018-03-14 07:26] http404error: In some cases, you can have a lot of mutual gain from design discussions *if* you have the capacity to recognize a good idea
[2018-03-14 07:26] Deleted User (Deleted User): tbh the "solution" if there is one. Multiple kids need to come up with cool content on their own
[2018-03-14 07:26] Deleted User (Deleted User): then, you can warn him for spamming if his shit dilutes the actual decent/non-trolly units
[2018-03-14 07:26] http404error: But the problem is, once you have that capacity, you're already capable of joining the cadre of competent designers
[2018-03-14 07:27] http404error: Do you think the current situation is acceptable? I think it's better, at least.
[2018-03-14 07:27] http404error: I'm actually pleasantly surprised at the number of users who understand the value of simplicity, so I haven't had to explain myself in that regard so much...
[2018-03-14 07:28] Deleted User (Deleted User): I think it's mildly annoying but pris discord is one of the few things I read everything of and generally I prefer more content to less
[2018-03-14 07:28] Deleted User (Deleted User): It could be argued not modding him is making it so less content is there
[2018-03-14 07:28] http404error: What I mean to ask is, do you think the design challenges are in effect a means of implementing what you think is the needed solution?
[2018-03-14 07:29] Deleted User (Deleted User): perhaps the unit design is just not a popular enough things for kids to mental masturbate over
[2018-03-14 07:29] Deleted User (Deleted User): maybe it will be in the future
[2018-03-14 07:30] http404error: It's hard to talk about bigger design principles in Prismata and have people actually follow along either.
[2018-03-14 07:30] Deleted User (Deleted User): if it is indeed not popular enough, i don't think you can really do anything about it short of putting a ton of effort into creating your own cool shit yourself and spending hours creating interesting content and/or a "narrative" or theme of the units/challenges
[2018-03-14 07:30] http404error: I honestly wonder if burninating the channel is fine too.
[2018-03-14 07:31] Deleted User (Deleted User): Atm I think the status quo is fine
[2018-03-14 07:31] Deleted User (Deleted User): Could be argued that that channel is bringing down the avg quality
[2018-03-14 07:33] Deleted User (Deleted User): you'd make a lot(1) of kids pretty disappointed
[2018-03-14 07:33] http404error: Productive game design requires a threshold of effort that most people can't be bothered to give
[2018-03-14 07:33] http404error: I thought yesterday's back-and-forth was really fun though
[2018-03-14 07:33] http404error: Actually got people talking a bit
[2018-03-14 07:34] http404error: I just don't know how to capture that
[2018-03-14 07:34] mqp: i also believe in moderating public forums such that if someone is not contributing positively, they stop contributing
[2018-03-14 07:34] mqp: it's not sufficient to be "posting in good faith" or "not breaking any rules" or whatever, you have to actively be saying something that is making the place net better for the other people there
[2018-03-14 07:35] http404error: Typically users naturally pick up the community spirit by absorbing feedback.
[2018-03-14 07:36] http404error: Somehow the Prismata community has attracted multiple people who are immune to said feedback.
[2018-03-14 07:36] mqp: every single async online community i've ever been a part of which i enjoyed either had moderation to accomplish this (e.g. metafilter, somethingawful, lesswrong) or was so small that all contributors did that without need for any moderation (e.g. people's blogs)
[2018-03-14 07:36] mqp: now it's actually true that i have been in some large chats that i enjoyed even though they were stupid and shitty so maybe chat is different somehow
[2018-03-14 07:38] mqp: if i were king, i would tell SSP to dominate the channel a little less and otherwise he seems fine, i like reading his units, i just don't like reading 10 of his units in a row with nothing in between
[2018-03-14 07:38] Deleted User (Deleted User): Yeah why don't we just tell him that

"personally, I have a hard time taking action against someone simply because the community thinks their opinions about the game are bad. if you imagine an alternate universe where every SSP unit was amazing and every SSP strategy tidbit was helpful, people would love him and this would not be an issue at all"

I would be more sympathetic towards SSP if he'd actually try the game and have some basis for his opinions even if they're wrong.  Although it's possible it would still feel like a problem in that case too idk.  As it is now I feel like SSP ought to be aware that he doesn't know what he's talking about...
[2018-03-14 07:40] http404error: I think workshopping units is a great use of the channel that's one actual advantage it has over reddit, but there's a clear inability to communicate bilaterally here
[2018-03-14 07:40] http404error: it reminds me a lot of a very thick language barrier
[2018-03-14 07:41] jamesa7171: oh, i fully agree with that @mtanzer and it frustrates me immensely that he has not tried the game. however, i secretly suspect that none of the problems people have with him would go away if he ever did play the game; SSP is apparently a grandmaster in CEO for instance and his community reputation there seems to be about the same
[2018-03-14 07:42] mqp: are you actually unhappy if apooche suggests 2 cool units in a day?
[2018-03-14 07:42] mqp: if not, then don't "make a rule" just so that you look "fair"
[2018-03-14 07:42] mqp: just use your judgment and tell people to cut it out when they overdo it
[2018-03-14 07:42] http404error: I've found it totally fruitless to try to impart some sense of "please stop posting as much" but I guess a couple more tries wouldn't hurt
[2018-03-14 07:43] mtanzer: yeah I have a feeling the problems with him wouldn't go away if he played the game.  I think I would be willing to put up with him a little longer before getting frustrated and wanting action taken against him but the end result would probably be the same

messages.content: now it's actually true that i have been in some large chats that i enjoyed even though they were stupid and shitty so maybe chat is different somehow

messages.content: Yeah why don't we just tell him that

messages.author: jamesa7171

messages.author: mqp
messages.content: just use your judgment and tell people to cut it out when they overdo it

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: I've found it totally fruitless to try to impart some sense of "please stop posting as much" but I guess a couple more tries wouldn't hurt

messages.author: mtanzer
messages.content: yeah I have a feeling the problems with him wouldn't go away if he played the game.  I think I would be willing to put up with him a little longer before getting frustrated and wanting action taken against him but the end result would probably be the same
